subject: A desolate volcanic mire under a sickly greenish sky, with steaming geothermal vents rising from black sulfurous pools and jagged dark ridgelines on the horizon
description: A wide, figureless landscape painting of a poisoned wetland. The foreground is a dark cracked plain pocked with bubbling pools and rising columns of pale steam or sulfurous smoke. Mid-ground, low jagged ridges of black-brown rock cut across the scene. The sky dominates: a roiling band of sickly yellow-green light bleeds across the horizon under heavy slate-grey clouds, evoking volcanic haze or swamp gas catching the last sun. No figures, no architecture, no creatures visible — just toxic geology. Palette is dominated by muddy browns, charcoal, and the bilious green-yellow sky.
foreground: cracked black mire with steaming vents and dark pools
background: jagged low ridges beneath a roiling green-yellow toxic sky with grey clouds
